calendar.matters:
Petition For Judicial Determination Of Subtrust Allocation, Interpretation Of Family Trust Terms, And Recovery Of Assets Improperly Allocated To The Survivor'S Trust
Off Calendar Feb-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PTR-23-306673, In the Matter of Perry L. and Gloria J. Wash Family Trust
PTR-24-307669, In the Matter of the Survivor's Trust of the Perry L. and Gloria J. Wash Family Trust

Appearances:
Milla Lvovich for Tanya Rockett, Executor/Respondent.
Nathan D. Borris and Jean Johnston for Martha E. Wash, Trustee/Petitioner.
Tanya Rockett and Martha Wash are present via Zoom.

Counsel presented oral arguments in support of their positions. Discussions were held regarding inconsistencies and contradicting items in the trust.

Mr. Borris offered to present newly obtained evidence to the court. Ms. Lvovich objected to the submission as hearsay. The court sustained Ms. Lvovich's objection.

The court suggested striking portions of the trust document relating to the special gift provision. Counsel was then instructed to meet and confer regarding the division of property.

The court took a brief recess to allow counsel to meet and confer as to property valuation and division of property. After meet and confer efforts counsel for both parties reported their progress. The court adopted Ms. Lvovich's proposed valuation and percentages for trust allocation.

Parties were instructed to finalize and submit a proposed order for review, which Mr. Borris confirmed he would do. The matter was taken under submission for final review.

Judge: Ross C. Moody/Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of An Amendment
Off Calendar Dec-04-2024 Off Calendar

Petition For Judicial Determination Of Subtrust Allocation, Interpretation Of Family Trust Terms, And Recovery Of Assets Improperly Allocated To The Survivor'S Trust
Off Calendar Dec-04-2024 Continued To Jan-17-25 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PES-22-305427, The Estate of Gloria Jean Wash Riley
PTR-23-306673, In the Matter of Perry L and Gloria J. Wash Family Trust
PTR-24-307669, In the Matter of the Survivor's Trust of the Perry L. and Gloria J. Wash Family Trust

Appearances (via Zoom):
Milla Lvovich for and with Tanya Rockett, Executor/Respondent.
Nathan D. Borris for Martha E. Wash, Trustee/Petitioner.

Discussion was held regarding estate matter 305427. Ms. Lvovich informed the court that she recently filed a Petition for Approval of Status Report and Request for Extension of Time to Continue Administration of the Estate, which is set for hearing on February 24, 2025. Counsel apologized to the court for missing the prior hearing and provided an explanation which the court found satisfactory. The court ordered the Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause off calendar and decided not to impose sanctions. The status on closing of estate hearing was also taken of calendar.

Further discussions were held in the trust matters (306673 and 307669). The court inquired about readiness for trial, case management, and discovery. Both counsel agreed there are no significant factual disputes, with issues primarily revolving around legal interpretations of trust documents. Counsel suggested the case may proceed based on briefing and oral argument without evidentiary hearings.

The court scheduled a court bench trial for January 17, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel were ordered to submit briefing and stipulated facts by January 10, 2025.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jul-17-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Borris appeared by zoom for Martha Wash (petitioner).
Mila Lvovich appeared by zoom for and with Tanya Rockett (petitioner/respondent) by zoom.

Mrs. Lvovich confirmed that they had mediation but were unable to settle the case. Mr. Borris stated that the stipulation to have the matters heard together is still pending and that they are ready to proceed/set an evidentiary hearing. The court informed counsel that a separate trust case number is needed for Trust A and stated that it is happy to adjudicate the family trust under this case, but if they want to litigate Trust A, they will need to file a different trust case. The court also spoke about requiring a joint state management statement at some point, and what it involves. The Petition for Judicial Determination of Subtrust Allocation, etc. is continued to September 11, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of an amendment and status on filing of a second trust case are also set for September 11, 2024. Mr. Borris stated that he will file a notice of related action. The new trust case/petition is to be given a hearing date of September 11, 2024.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Belen. Not reported.

Petition For Judicial Determination Of Subtrust Allocation, For Order Confirming Effect Of Exercise Of Power Of Appointment, For Order Confirming Distributive Shares To Beneficiaries, And For Order Determining Vadility Of Trust Amendement
Off Calendar Jul-17-2024 Continued To Sep-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Judicial Determination Of Subtrust Allocation, For Order Confirming Effect Of Exercise Of Power Of Appointment, For Order Confirming Distributive Shares To Beneficiaries, And For Order Determining Vadility Of Trust Amendement
Off Calendar Apr-15-2024 Continued To Jul-17-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Borris appeared by zoom for Martha Wash (petitioner).
Andrea Lovrincevic appeared by zoom for and with Tanya Rockett by zoom.

Mr. Borris spoke of the examiner's suggestion of splitting the case and stated that they filed a stipulation regarding consolidation of this. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than June 28, 2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by July 5, 2024. The Petition for Judicial Determination of Subtrust Allocation, etc., is continued to and the Parties are to return on July 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are to obtain a copy of the mediation order from the court's website.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-03-2024 Continued To Feb-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Nathan D. Borris, Esq., via Zoom, for and with Martha E. Wash, Trustee.
Milla Lvovich, Esq., in court, for and with Tanya Rockett, Respondent.

At the court's query, counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding whether venue is proper. Counsel advises of a related Estate matter (PES-22-305427) currently set for hearing on Jun-10-2024.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Feb-05-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross. C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Petrucci. Not Reported.
